response.justification: Bogdan has a strong and diverse technical background spanning 15+ years with relevant exposure to developer tooling, CI/CD infrastructure, and test frameworks, but this role is explicitly a people-leadership position and the resume shows zero people management experience. There is no evidence of managing engineers, conducting performance reviews, setting team priorities, or leading organizational change — all of which are core requirements. His Staff Engineer titles indicate senior IC scope, not team leadership. The technical foundation is genuinely solid, but the management gap is fundamental and disqualifying for a Level II Manager role.
strengths: Genuine developer tooling and testing infrastructure experience: built open-source test runners (Trial), assertion libraries (Fluent-Asserts), CI/CD pipelines, and MCP code generators
Broad technical range across web, mobile, embedded, and systems programming with a consistent emphasis on TDD, code quality, and craftsmanship practices
Some teaching and knowledge-sharing initiatives (Selenium courses, Code Kata sessions, conference talks) that signal mentorship instincts
concerns: No people management experience anywhere in the resume — no mention of direct reports, performance cycles, hiring, or team development
No evidence of organizational leadership, roadmap ownership, or cross-team strategic alignment at a program or department level
Mobile platform depth is superficial (one iOS/tvOS stint in 2015–2016); the role requires sustained mobile ecosystem expertise to credibly lead productivity tooling for a mobile platform

key_factors.factor: Developer tooling and testing infrastructure background
No organizational leadership or change management
